text: Developing a mobile app involves several important choices. One key decision is responsive design, ensuring your app works well on different screen sizes and orientations. Another is whether to build a native app (made specifically for iOS or Android) or a cross-platform app (which works on both using shared code). Native apps often perform better and access device features more smoothly, while cross-platform development saves time and money. Once the app is built, you submit it to app stores like Apple’s App Store or Google Play, where it undergoes review. Good performance—fast loading, smooth navigation, and low battery usage—is essential for user satisfaction and positive reviews.
